symbols.name: E
symbols.def: E has N(0,b) law and is independent of Z

symbols.name: delta_n
symbols.def: a sequence indexed by n with delta_n downarrow 0

symbols.name: psi_l
symbols.def: the normalized probabilists' Hermite basis of L2(N(0,1))

symbols.name: P_{h,delta}
symbols.def: P_{h,delta}=P_{+,delta} if h=+ and P_{h,delta}=P_{-,delta} if h=-

symbols.name: s_P
symbols.def: s_P=grad log p for a positive C2 density p of P

symbols.name: J_j(P)
symbols.def: J_j(P)=partial_j s_{P,j}(X,Y) for (X,Y) distributed as P

symbols.name: v_h
symbols.def: v_h=partial_delta log p_{h,delta} evaluated at delta=0

symbols.name: C_star(delta)
symbols.role: Chernoff information at tilt one half

symbols.name: P_{h,delta,g_n}
symbols.def: the law P_{h,delta} obtained by inserting g_n in f_delta

symbols.name: R_n(delta)
symbols.def: inf_pi max_h P_{h,delta}^n{pi != h}, with pi Borel from (R^2)^n to {+,-}

symbols.name: D_n^H
symbols.def: D_n^H=+ if widehat Delta_n^H is nonnegative and D_n^H=- otherwise

novel.justification: A nondegenerate correlated Gaussian center is exactly invariant under exchange of X and Y and is the reversible intersection of the two local arrows.
Exact removal of the affine Hermite components isolates departure from the linear-Gaussian reversible submodel without choosing an oracle nonlinear direction.

assumptions.condition: max_{r in {0,1,2,3}} sup_{z in R} exp(|z|/8)|g^(r)(z)|<=B.

novel.justification: Gaussian-windowed sinusoidal and compactly supported spline mechanisms satisfy this numerical envelope. It makes the unbounded Gaussian design compatible with uniform score-derivative concentration.
This excludes the affine reversible submodel while admitting smooth nonlinear mechanisms with a declared minimum curvature scale; it is a class regularity radius, not a testing conclusion.

definitions.name: Gaussian-Hermite mechanism class
definitions.construction: G_s(L,B,kappa)={g=sum_{l=0}^infinity theta_l psi_l : ass:hermite-centering, ass:hermite-sobolev, ass:weighted-envelope, and ass:curvature-radius hold, with s in (9/4,infinity)}.

definitions.name: score-Jacobian variance map
definitions.construction: For every positive C2 law P and j in {x,y}, set s_P=grad log p, J_j(P)=partial_j s_{P,j}(X,Y), and V_j(P)=Var_P[J_j(P)].

definitions.name: antisymmetric likelihood-ratio equalizer
definitions.construction: For every (n,delta), pi_{n,delta}^{LR} compares product_i p_{+,delta}(X_i,Y_i) with product_i p_{-,delta}(X_i,Y_i); on equality it uses one fixed Borel tie rule a_n satisfying a_n((y_i,x_i)_i)=-a_n((x_i,y_i)_i) off the fixed-point set.

definitions.name: symmetric cross-fitted Hermite-sieve score-Jacobian learner
definitions.construction: For each n,k and each orientation u in {x,y}, on observations outside I_{n,k}, least-squares fit the conditional mean of the other coordinate on u over span{psi_0,...,psi_{m_n^H}}, estimate its residual variance, form the corresponding additive-Gaussian log-density and its diagonal score-Jacobian. Select between the two fitted log-densities by their training Hyvarinen score with deterministic exchange-equivariant ties, call the selected density widehat p_k^H, and set widehat J_{j,k}^H=partial_j^2 log widehat p_k^H. On I_{n,k}, set widehat V_{j,k}^H=|I_{n,k}|^(-1)sum_{i in I_{n,k}}(widehat J_{j,k}^H(X_i,Y_i)-|I_{n,k}|^(-1)sum_{ell in I_{n,k}}widehat J_{j,k}^H(X_ell,Y_ell))^2 and widehat Delta_n^H=2^(-1)sum_k(widehat V_{x,k}^H-widehat V_{y,k}^H).

definitions.name: tilted score-contrast mistake losses
definitions.construction: For delta_n downarrow 0 with n C_m(delta_n)->infinity, let L_n=sum_i ell_{delta_n}(X_i,Y_i), K_{+,n}=E_{m_n^n}[exp(L_n/2)1{D_n^H=-}], and K_{-,n}=E_{m_n^n}[exp(-L_n/2)1{D_n^H=+}]. Handle: characterize primitive stability of def:hermite-sieve-learner sufficient for log max(K_{+,n},K_{-,n})=o(n C_star(delta_n)).

definitions.name: common-map rare-event nuisance perturbation
definitions.construction: For any local sequence satisfying n C_star(delta_n)/log log(n+e)->infinity, take q_n=1/log(n+e), distinct training observations i(k), and A_{n,k}={X_{i(k)}>Phi^(-1)(1-q_n)}. Starting from a common data-measurable map, on A_{n,k} replace its y diagonal output by M_n X and otherwise retain it. The schedule q_n and positive M_n are deterministic functions of n and independent of h, delta_n, g, and P.

statements.statement: For every g in G_s(L,B,kappa) and every sufficiently small delta != 0, P_{+,delta} is the additive-Gaussian ANM X->Y and P_{-,delta} is its exchanged Y->X mirror. V_y(P_{+,delta})=V_x(P_{-,delta})=0 and V_x(P_{+,delta})=V_y(P_{-,delta})=C_m(delta)>0. As delta -> 0, C_m(delta)=c_1 delta^2+o(delta^2), c_1>0, and C_star(delta)=(c_2/8)delta^2+o(delta^2)=(c_2/(8c_1))C_m(delta)+o(C_m(delta)), c_2>0.
statements.justification: It derives the intrinsic likelihood scale from SCORE's native diagonal-Jacobian gap uniformly over an explicit unknown-mechanism class.
statements.gap: Rolland et al. give population leaf identification and Zhu et al. give a sufficient estimation guarantee; neither maps C_m to Chernoff information at the linear-Gaussian center over an unknown mechanism class.
statements.consumer: It supplies the information benchmark against which any adaptive score learner and any causal-order procedure can be calibrated.

statements.statement: For each fixed g in G_s(L,B,kappa) and fixed sufficiently small delta != 0, R_n(delta)=exp{-n[C_star(delta)+o_n(1)]}. Consequently lim_(delta downarrow 0)lim_(n->infinity)[-log R_n(delta)]/[n C_m(delta)]=c_2/(8c_1).
statements.justification: This converts the local geometry into the likelihood-optimal binary order-testing frontier.
statements.gap: Chernoff supplies generic simple-testing theory, but no prior result indexes its ANM order-risk exponent by SCORE's C_m.
statements.consumer: It is the oracle benchmark that reveals the cost of adapting to an unknown mechanism.

statements.statement: For every (n,delta), R_n(delta)=P_{+,delta}^n{pi_{n,delta}^{LR}!=+}=P_{-,delta}^n{pi_{n,delta}^{LR}!=-}. Along every delta_n downarrow 0, E_{m_n}[ell_{delta_n}(X,Y)^2]=O(delta_n^2). If n C_m(delta_n)->rho in [0,infinity), then R_n(delta_n)->Phi(-0.5 sqrt(rho c_2/c_1)); if n C_m(delta_n)->infinity, then [-log R_n(delta_n)]/[n C_star(delta_n)]->1 and [-log R_n(delta_n)]/[n C_m(delta_n)]->c_2/(8c_1). These are pointwise in the fixed mirror path.
statements.justification: The equalizer identity gives the finite-information elbow and the exact supercritical likelihood benchmark.
statements.gap: Qualitative ANM identification does not furnish moving-alternative directional risk limits around the reversible center.
statements.consumer: It distinguishes information scarcity from adaptive estimation scarcity in the proposed frontier.

statements.statement: At delta=0, P_{+,0}=P_{-,0}=P_0 and R_n(0)=1/2 for every n.
statements.justification: The mirror experiment must reduce to known linear-Gaussian observational equivalence.
statements.gap: Existing ANM identification results identify this exception but do not embed it in a quantitative decision frontier.
statements.consumer: It anchors the local asymptotics at genuine nonidentification.

statements.statement: The threshold s>9/4 is sufficient for L4-consistent estimation of the diagonal score Jacobian on G_s(L,B,kappa). For the common, exchange-equivariant learner in def:hermite-sieve-learner, uniformly over h in {+,-} and g in G_s(L,B,kappa), ||widehat J_{j,k}^H-J_j(P_{h,delta_n})||_{L4(P_{h,delta_n})}=O_P(r_n) and its centered empirical contrast has an exponential deviation bound P_{h,delta_n}^n{|widehat Delta_n^H-E[widehat Delta_n^H|G_{n,k}]|>t}<=2 exp{-a n min(t^2/(delta_n^4+r_n^4),t/(delta_n^2+r_n^2))} for numerical a>0. Thus, if n C_m(delta_n)->infinity and delta_n/r_n->infinity, sup_{g in G_s(L,B,kappa)}max_h P_{h,delta_n}^n{D_n^H!=h}->0.
statements.justification: This states an explicit law- and direction-independent construction and derives its estimation-limited local regime from numeric primitives rather than an oracle nuisance assumption.
statements.gap: Kpotufe et al. establish generic ANM consistency, Zhu et al. assume score-estimation control for SCORE, and score-discovery papers provide algorithms or population conditions; none gives an L4 derivative rate plus exponential score-contrast bound over an unknown smooth mechanism class at the near-reversible boundary.
statements.consumer: It yields a checkable sample-size condition for a score-Jacobian order detector in applications with a declared mechanism smoothness budget.

statements.statement: Let A_n be any common, law- and direction-independent, exchange-equivariant score-Jacobian learner or score-contrast detector whose order decision is measurable with respect to its cross-fitted diagonal-Jacobian outputs. There are constants eps,c>0 and, for every sequence delta_n with n delta_n^2->infinity and delta_n/r_n<=c, mechanisms g_n in G_s(L,B,kappa), constructed from disjoint Hermite shells after centering, such that max_h P_{h,delta_n,g_n}^n{A_n!=h}>=eps. Hence the uniform adaptive score-contrast boundary is delta_n asymptotically larger than r_n, even though the known-g likelihood rule retains the linear-C_m Chernoff frontier.
statements.justification: The lower bound separates the intrinsic directional information scale from the nonparametric cost of learning the score curvature without knowing g.
statements.gap: Koehler, Heckett, and Risteski show that score matching can lose efficiency in other distributional geometries, but do not supply an unknown-mechanism causal-order lower bound. No SCORE paper proves this adaptation separation.
statements.consumer: It prevents interpreting the oracle Chernoff frontier as a uniform feasibility claim for score-based causal discovery and identifies the complexity price a learner must pay.

statements.statement: Can a concrete common, law- and direction-independent score-Jacobian training procedure attain log max(K_{+,n},K_{-,n})=o(n C_star(delta_n)) on any nontrivial subset of the estimation-limited regime, and what structural restriction beyond G_s(L,B,kappa) is necessary for coefficient-one Chernoff efficiency?
statements.justification: The adaptive boundary establishes consistency but does not establish tangent correlation one or a likelihood exponent for the sieve detector.
statements.gap: No cited score-based causal-order result verifies tilted mistake-loss control for a concrete learner near a reversible ANM boundary.
statements.consumer: It isolates the remaining route from an adaptive consistency frontier to a certified likelihood-efficient score procedure.

estimand_functional: C_star(delta)=(c_2/(8c_1))C_m(delta)+o(C_m(delta)); [n C_m(delta_n)->infinity and delta_n/r_n->infinity] => sup_(g in G_s(L,B,kappa)) max_(h in {+,-}) P_{h,delta_n}^n{D_n^H!=h}->0

sampling_model.treatment: no treatment; h indexes the causal order in a two-point structural experiment
sampling_model.overlap: both mirror densities are positive and share P_0 as delta tends to zero
sampling_model.nuisance_estimation: The only feasible construction is def:hermite-sieve-learner. Its tuning depends on n and (s,L,B,kappa), never on h, P, g, delta, C_m, C_star, or a likelihood tangent.

bibliography.citation: Rolland, P., Cevher, V., Kleindessner, M., Russell, C., Schölkopf, B., Janzing, D., and Locatello, F. (2022). SCORE. ICML/PMLR 162.
Zhu, Z., Locatello, F., and Cevher, V. (2023). Sample complexity of causal discovery using score matching. NeurIPS.
Hoyer, P., Janzing, D., Mooij, J., Peters, J., and Schölkopf, B. (2008). Nonlinear causal discovery with additive noise models. NeurIPS.
Peters, J., Mooij, J., Janzing, D., and Schölkopf, B. (2014). Causal discovery with continuous additive noise models. JMLR 15.
Kpotufe, S., Sgouritsa, E., Janzing, D., and Schölkopf, B. (2014). Consistency of causal inference under the additive noise model. ICML/PMLR 32.
Montagna, F., Noceti, N., Rosasco, L., Zhang, K., and Locatello, F. (2023). Causal discovery with score matching on additive models with arbitrary noise. CLeaR.
Montagna, F., Noceti, N., Rosasco, L., Zhang, K., and Locatello, F. (2023). Scalable causal discovery with score matching. arXiv:2304.03382.
Montagna, F., Noceti, N., Rosasco, L., and Locatello, F. (2023). Shortcuts for causal discovery of nonlinear models by score matching. arXiv:2310.14246.
Montagna, F., Faller, P., Blöbaum, P., Kirschbaum, E., and Locatello, F. (2025). Score matching through the roof. CLeaR/PMLR 275.
Koehler, F., Heckett, A., and Risteski, A. (2023). Statistical efficiency of score matching. ICLR.
Chernoff, H. (1952). A measure of asymptotic efficiency for tests of a hypothesis. Annals of Mathematical Statistics 23.
Tsybakov, A. (2009). Introduction to Nonparametric Estimation. Springer.

comparator_promise_table.comparator_claim: Population SCORE identifies leaves in nonlinear additive Gaussian-noise models from score-Jacobian structure.
Finite-sample SCORE correctness follows from score-estimation control and a curvature margin.
Nonlinear ANMs generically distinguish causal direction apart from reversible exceptions.
Continuous ANMs identify a DAG under mild population conditions.
A family of ANM causal-inference procedures is consistent under nonparametric conditions.
NoGAM addresses generic-noise additive models and Gaussian-score edge inversions.
DAS uses score derivatives for scalable causal-graph discovery in additive Gaussian models.
Score-sortability characterizes an overlapping bivariate ANM class and studies ScoreSort.
Score-based conditions and algorithms cover nonlinear and latent-variable causal discovery.
Score matching can be comparable to or less efficient than likelihood methods depending on geometry.
Chernoff information characterizes simple product-measure testing exponents.

tldr: For the explicitly defined two-node mirror additive-Gaussian path, the project keeps the pointwise C_m-to-Chernoff geometry and asks a different question: what is lost when the nonlinear mechanism is unknown? A Gaussian-Hermite class and an explicit symmetric cross-fitted sieve learner yield a proposed estimation-limited score-contrast boundary delta/r_n -> infinity, while a Hermite-shell lower bound targets the converse despite likelihood-supercritical n C_m -> infinity. The likelihood geometry, fixed-alternative frontier, and local elbow remain pointwise benchmark results; coefficient-one Chernoff efficiency of a concrete score learner remains open.

project_justification.gap: Existing ANM and SCORE results do not distinguish information-theoretic order recovery from the uniform cost of estimating a diagonal score Jacobian when the mechanism is unknown near the linear-Gaussian boundary.
project_justification.niche: The frontier concerns a transparent smooth mechanism class, an orientation-blind score learner, and a local causal-order decision, rather than generic density estimation or an oracle-known mechanism.
project_justification.fill: The project derives a common-map Hermite-sieve upper boundary and a matching Hermite-shell adaptation lower bound while retaining the mirror C_m-to-Chernoff benchmark as the oracle comparison.

related_work: Rolland et al. establish SCORE's population leaf criterion; their object is not a local unknown-mechanism frontier. Zhu, Locatello, and Cevher give a finite-sample sufficient SCORE guarantee; they do not derive a common-map diagonal-Jacobian rate or an adaptive lower boundary. Hoyer et al. and Peters et al. give qualitative additive-noise identification, and Kpotufe et al. give nonparametric consistency conditions; none quantifies the cost of approaching the reversible center with unknown g. Chernoff supplies the binary testing benchmark, not the C_m calibration or adaptation result. Montagna et al.'s NoGAM changes the noise regime, DAS develops scalable score-derivative graph discovery, Shortcuts studies score-sortability, and Score Matching through the Roof develops broader score identification; each has a different population or algorithmic object from the mirror-path adaptive frontier. Koehler, Heckett, and Risteski motivate the likelihood-versus-score comparison but do not treat causal-order recovery or a Hermite-shell lower bound.
technical_internal_limitation: Supporting diagnostic only, not a contribution and not a claim about SCORE or any published estimator: arbitrary training-random O_P(L4) control of a newly defined cross-fitted template cannot itself give a uniform Chernoff-exponent fraction. On the faster diagnostic subsequence n C_star(delta_n)/log log(n+e)->infinity, the common-map construction in def:rare-event-nuisance-perturbation has independent P_+ triggers of probability q_n and P_- trigger probabilities tending to zero. It preserves L4 O_P containment because q_n->0, but P_+^n{D_n=-}>=q_n^2, so 0<=-log P_+^n{D_n=-}/[n C_star(delta_n)]<=2 log log(n+e)/[n C_star(delta_n)]->0. Equivalently P_+^n{D_n=-}=exp[-n C_star(delta_n)]K_{+,n} and P_-^n{D_n=+}=exp[-n C_star(delta_n)]K_{-,n}. This does not say every explicit stable learner fails.
honest_scope: The C_m-to-Chernoff map, equalizer, and elbow are pointwise for each fixed admissible mirror path; no uniform global nonlinear-ANM constant is claimed because c_1 and c_2 have no lower envelopes. The new uniform statements are restricted to G_s(L,B,kappa), the displayed score-contrast detector class, and likelihood-supercritical sequences. The proposed sieve result does not claim a Chernoff exponent, and primitive tilted-loss conditions for coefficient-one efficiency remain open. The diagnostic rare-event construction is confined to its faster subsequence and weak O_P premise. At delta=0 the model reduces to linear-Gaussian nonidentification.

novelty_justification.published_axis: No close paper combines the mirror Gaussian-ANM C_m-to-Chernoff map with a uniform unknown-g adaptation lower bound for diagonal score-Jacobian order recovery. The proposal neither tightens Zhu et al.'s general SCORE guarantee nor refutes published SCORE; it asks a distinct complexity question for one explicit learner and detector class.
novelty_justification.repo_axis: The carried field-tier mirror likelihood kernel is retained, but the new theorem-level object is the mechanism-adaptation separation between likelihood-supercritical recovery and score-Jacobian learning.

literature_checklist.author: Rolland et al.
literature_checklist.one_line: Population SCORE leaf identification from score-Jacobian structure in additive Gaussian-noise models.

literature_checklist.author: Zhu, Locatello, and Cevher
literature_checklist.one_line: Finite-sample SCORE guarantee based on score estimation and a curvature margin, without a local minimax converse.

literature_checklist.author: Chernoff
literature_checklist.one_line: Product-measure binary-testing exponent used for the mirror likelihood benchmark.

literature_checklist.author: Kpotufe et al.
literature_checklist.one_line: Consistency theory for additive-noise causal inference without a near-reversible unknown-mechanism score-learning frontier.

literature_checklist.author: Koehler, Heckett, and Risteski
literature_checklist.one_line: Likelihood-versus-score efficiency comparisons outside causal-order adaptation.

literature_checklist.author: Montagna et al.
literature_checklist.one_line: Generic-noise score-based causal discovery, distinct from the Gaussian mirror-path adaptation frontier.
